title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong had a crucial 3-RBI game in the Red Sox win against Cleveland, impacting his position on the team.
Wong's performance included a disputed home run initially celebrated but later ruled a double after review.
Interim manager Chad Tracy named Mickey Gasper the primary catcher, with Wong competing for secondary duties.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings with seven strikeouts in the Red Sox's 9-1 victory over the Guardians.
Gray allowed one run on four hits and three walks, lowering his season ERA to 3.06.
Gray's performance in May included a 2.00 ERA over five starts and only one home run in 27 innings.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye is hosting a charity softball game at Polar Park in Worcester on Sunday.
Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels will lead Team Patriots and Team Sox, respectively.
The event features athletes like Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, and comedians Matt Rife and Dave Portnoy.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The New England Patriots are reportedly close to acquiring Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown, with a trade framework in place.
Trade is likely to be finalized post-June 1 to reduce the Eagles' salary cap hit from $43.5 million to $16 million.
Negotiations continue as Patriots resist giving a first-round pick, but might agree to secure Brown's services.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud boom was heard across Greater Boston on Saturday afternoon, with reports flooding social media and local officials.
Meteorologists dismissed an earthquake and active thunderstorms as causes, suggesting a bolide meteor as the likely source.
Reports of the boom prompted numerous 911 calls, but no fires or explosions were found by responding authorities.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Opening pitcher Tyler Samaniego allowed all four runs in the Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
Boston Red Sox fell to 23-33, last in the AL East, after losing six of their last seven games.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans for their passion at the TD Garden during a recent livestream.
Boston and Miami Heat are top contenders for trading with Antetokounmpo, who wants to stay in the East.
Speculation includes a possible trade involving Jaylen Brown for Antetokounmpo, with Boston considering various trade scenarios.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed Harvard's annual Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd and safety concerns.
Massachusetts State, Cambridge, and Harvard police were involved in clearing the bridge after reports of path blockage.
The event followed Harvard's commencement, where Conan O'Brien spoke, and no arrests or injuries were immediately reported.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now available for streaming on Peacock.
'The Truman Show' featuring Jim Carrey is streaming on Paramount+ and YouTube.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2 starring Tina Fey is available on Netflix.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts the Red Sox will draft 18-year-old Bo Lowrance, a third baseman from South Carolina.
The Red Sox are projected to select Lowrance with the 20th pick in the July 11, 2026 MLB Draft.
Lowrance, compared to Freddie Freeman, has committed to the University of Virginia and could switch to first base.
